definition: Conferences and in-person events are high-leverage channels for founder fundraising and audience access due to face-to-face trust compression.
rationale: Coherent concept with clean centroid; all three members align on the core insight (conferences/events as leverage mechanism). Minimal variance (0.062).

definition: Investor confidence scales with the number of assumptions they must believe; build thesis around provably-true de-risked conditions, not aspirational facts.
rationale: Both members converge on the core lever (reducing assumption load), not restating investor psychology. High similarity (0.959) indicates tight semantic overlap.

definition: Well-designed process (scripts, checklists, repeatable steps) reduces skill requirement and enables junior talent to execute consistently at scale.
rationale: Proposed name (Script Optimization) is implementation detail, not the load-bearing concept. Core insight is that process substitutes for talent/specialization; rename to capture the mechanism.

definition: Success in one domain transfers to others because underlying skill-acquisition mechanisms are domain-agnostic; top performers apply this transitive property naturally.
rationale: Both members assert the same mechanism (skill transfer across domains via universal learning principles). Tight variance (0.032) and high similarity (0.968).

canonical_name: Brand as Escape from Performance Marketing

splits.definition: Defensible brand via customer belief and ambassador evangelism (not patents or arbitrage) commands premium pricing and builds durable competitive advantage.
Strong brand directly shifts customer choice and willingness-to-pay, driving repeat purchases and lifetime value.

definition: Brand is the escape valve from performance marketing trap (low margins, commoditized). Drives premium pricing, retention, and defensibility.
rationale: Cluster conflates two distinct load-bearing ideas: (1) brand as moat/competitive advantage, (2) brand as behavioral lever on customer choice. Split to clarify. Talent Retention outlier stays in parent (brand loyalty drives retention too).

definition: Vertically integrating wholesale operations in-house gives you access to their full volume, not just their shared fraction. Cash flow from wholesale funds the high-multiple operations.
rationale: Proposed name (Fund vs. Operations) is structural framing. Core insight is the leverage gain from integration; rename to clarify the mechanism (full volume capture).

definition: All operational failure is recoverable through iteration unless you die; reframe failure as a solvable iteration problem, not a terminal event.
rationale: Both members assert the same provocative claim (mortality as the only real failure). Near-perfect overlap (0.981 similarity); minimal variance (0.019).

definition: Mature behavior decouples feeling from action; you can feel afraid/angry/uncertain while acting normally. Identity is determined by action, not emotion.
rationale: All three members converge on the same mechanism (emotional regulation via decoupling). Centroid is clean; variance (0.051) indicates tight thematic alignment.

canonical_name: Learning Requires Behavior Change Through Feedback
definition: Learning is measurable only as behavior change in the same condition, proved by entering that condition again and responding differently. Content consumption without behavior change is entertainment.
rationale: Four members all assert the same operational definition of learning (Skinnerian: condition constant, response changes). High cross-source agreement (2 sources on one headline). Tight cluster semantically.

Cluster is over-heterogeneous (variance 0.087, min_intra_sim 0.899—the weakest in batch). Members span three unrelated concepts: (1) ignorance-is-not-defense, (2) franchise legal structure, (3) regulatory gray zones. No load-bearing concept holds them together. Kick and rebuild as three separate L0 concepts.

canonical_name: Close Rate as Pricing Calibration Signal
aliases: Close Rate Analysis and Price Optimization
definition: Close rate reveals pricing power: 80%+ signals underpricing (3-4x room), 30-40% is calibrated, <30% indicates sales/qualification problem not pricing.
rationale: Four members all leverage the same axis (close rate as pricing feedback loop). Proposed canonical is clean; members add quantified anchors (80%, 30-40%). Variance (0.049) is tight.

definition: Identify situations with bounded downside and uncapped upside; young person with time has capped loss (zero) and unlimited attempts. Arbitrage lives in the gap between downside and upside.
rationale: Proposed name (Overestimating Downside Risk) is problem framing, not the core insight. Cluster is really about recognizing asymmetric-bet shapes and acting on them. Rename to framework name.

canonical_name: Time Allocation Across Work and Skill-Building
definition: Structure time blocks: day job pays present expenses (8am–5pm); morning/evening blocks (5–9am, 5–9pm) build future. Reclaim ~4-hour windows before/after employment for skill-building.
rationale: Three members all propose time-slicing tactics with the same logic (present vs. future). Variance (0.041) is low; all align on the 4-hour block pattern.

definition: Capture value by pre-processing complexity and delivering simple output; the fortune is in doing the invisible work (research, distillation) so the customer receives effortless results.
rationale: Three members converge on the same lever (pre-work compression). Centroid is clean; members provide illustrative angles (pre-chewed food, magical product feeling). Variance (0.059) is acceptable.

splits.definition: Ask audience survey: where did you first hear about us? Use qualitative results to identify and power-rank the primary discovery channel.

splits.canonical_name: ICP Avatar Definition via Customer Language
splits.definition: Define ICP phase 1 as emotional testimonials from representative customers at $15-25k upfront price point, using their native language for positioning.

definition: Simplest attribution method: ask customers where they first discovered you. Use qualitative directional signal (not pixel tracking) to allocate media spend.
rationale: Cluster mixes two unrelated tactics: (1) qualitative attribution for channel discovery, (2) customer avatar definition via testimonial language. 'Offer Bifurcation' is semantically remote (0.905 sim). Split to separate channel insights from positioning insights.
